pi_items.source: Task description line 1 and export_result.sh line 18 (searches for this channel name)
Task description: 'Receive HL7 v2.x messages via TCP/MLLP on port 6661'; setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 84 exposes port 6661; HL7 standard listener port
Task description: 'Forward messages to a downstream system at localhost:6665'; setup_task.sh lines 44 (receiver listens on 0.0.0.0:6665); verifier.py line 94 checks for localhost/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0
Task description: 'Forward messages to a downstream system at localhost:6665'; setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 88 exposes port 6665; setup_task.sh line 45 creates Downstream_ADT_Receiver on port 6665
Task description: 'Configure Destination Queuing to ensure reliability: set Queue Messages to Always'; verifier.py line 115-117 checks queueEnabled=true, sendFirst=false
Task description requires 'Queue Messages' to 'Always' (not 'On Failure'); verifier.py line 115 checks sendFirst=false when queueEnabled=true
Task description: 'Retry Interval to 10000ms'; NextGen Connect documentation confirms default retry interval is 10000 ms; verifier.py line 126 checks retryIntervalMillis=10000
Task description: 'Max Retry Count to 5'; verifier.py line 134 checks retryCount=5
Task description: 'enable Rotate Queue'; verifier.py line 142 checks rotate=true; Rotate Queue is a standard TCP Sender feature in NextGen Connect for managing queue failures
setup_task.sh line 13 creates HL7 message with MSH segment showing version 2.5.1; HL7 v2.5.1 is a real, published standard for healthcare messaging
setup_task.sh line 13 message type ADT^A01^ADT_A01; ADT_A01 is the Admit/Visit Notification trigger event in HL7 standard (verified via Caristix HL7-Definition v2 documentation)

Task description: 'via TCP/MLLP'; setup_task.sh lines 61-69 configure MLLP transmission mode with startOfMessageBytes=0B (0x0B), endOfMessageBytes=1C0D (0x1C0D); MLLP (Minimum Lower Layer Protocol) is the standard for HL7 message framing over TCP/IP
setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 98 uses Docker image nextgenhealthcare/connect:4.5.0; verifier.py line 2 references version 4.5.0
Task description: 'A sample ADT message is available at /home/ga/test_adt_a01.hl7'; setup_task.sh lines 12-19 creates this file
setup_task.sh line 15 PID segment contains SSN987654321; this is synthetic (repeating digit pattern, not valid real SSN)
setup_task.sh lines 15-17 contain phone numbers 2175551234, 2175555678, 2175559876; 555 exchange is reserved by NANPA for use in fiction/TV (not real)
setup_task.sh lines 14-17 use generic test names (DOE is standard test surname); not based on real individuals

privileged_info_summary: Task 'tcp_sender_queue_config@1' configures a NextGen Connect 4.5.0 channel named 'ADT_Message_Relay' for HL7 v2.5.1 healthcare message integration. The channel receives ADT_A01 admission notifications via TCP/MLLP on port 6661 and forwards them to localhost:6665 with destination queuing configured for reliability (queue always, 10000ms retry interval, 5 max retries, rotate queue enabled). All metadata configuration parameters are verified against task requirements and NextGen Connect documentation. Test data is entirely synthetic (HL7 message uses 555 phone exchange, invalid SSN pattern, generic test names like DOE/JANE).
